title: crotalaria semperflorens var. walkeri
intro: Crotalaria semperflorens var. walkeri, also known as Walkers Crotalaria, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the pea family and is native to tropical regions. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its high alkaloid content. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In traditional medicine, Walkers Crotalaria is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves and flowers are used to make infusions and salves for topical application.
